title: King Ponthus and the Fair Sidone
summary: CME00068 is the repository copy of a University of Michigan electronic transcription of Frank Jewett Mather Jr.'s 1897 edition of the anonymous Middle English King Ponthus and the Fair Sidone. The encoding covers Mather's edited text and apparatus on printed pp. 1-150, not his introductory pp. i-lxvii or facsimile plate. Mather transcribes Digby 185 as his base, prints two Douce 384 fragments in notes, and supplies a passage corresponding to printed p. 57 after line 13 through p. 60 line 13 from the later 1511 W text because Digby's exemplar had lost a leaf. A newly current complete 424-surface Digby digital object is cached and mapped to ff. 166r-203r / canvases 339-413; its older thirteen-image Digital Bodleian object remains a separately modeled legacy partial selection. No complete public Douce 384 object was found, so the record preserves a dated whole-composite negative result and Bodleian reproduction-order route. The Mather volume, Cambridge public front matter, Bodleian catalog, and complete Ortuin printed French comparison copy are also cached with exact boundaries.
review_notes: The anonymous English work, its physical witnesses, a later English printed version, French source work and comparison witnesses, Mather's edition, embedded plate, digital reproductions, U-M encoding, and repository artifact are distinct entities.
A complete physical-object surrogate is retained whenever publicly obtainable. Dated whole-object negative results and official request routes are recorded for every manuscript witness lacking one.
Caching research files does not grant redistribution or print-on-demand rights; image licenses and underlying-text status remain separate layers.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00068
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID identify CME00068; the file is 408,880 bytes.
The XML has one DIV1, thirty-five DIV2 chapters, 150 PB elements, 305 notes, 368 paragraphs, and 986 milestones.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, CME00068
entities.description: A manually keyed electronic text derived from Mather's printed Middle English text and notes on pp. 1-150, not a fresh direct transcription from manuscript images.

entities.label: Frank Jewett Mather Jr., King Ponthus and the Fair Sidone (1897)
entities.description: The first publication of the Digby-based Middle English version. Mather's contribution includes preface and introduction on printed pp. i-lxvii, an unnumbered Digby f. 166r plate, and edited text with apparatus on pp. 1-150.

bibliographic.citation: Frank Jewett Mather Jr., 'King Ponthus and the Fair Sidone,' Publications of the Modern Language Association of America 12.1 (1897): i-lxvii, 1-150, doi:10.2307/456284.
bibliographic.title: King Ponthus and the Fair Sidone
bibliographic.container_title: Publications of the Modern Language Association of America

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of PMLA volume 12 (1897)
entities.description: A complete 628-page scan of the bound PMLA volume. Mather's complete contribution occupies PDF pp. 13-234; its edited Middle English text occupies PDF pp. 85-234.

entities.label: Cambridge Core public PMLA 12.1 front matter PDF
entities.description: A current public sixty-five-page PDF reproducing Mather's printed pp. iii-lxvii. It omits preface pp. i-ii, the Digby plate, and the edited Middle English text pp. 1-150.

entities.label: Mather's facsimile plate of Digby 185, f. 166r
entities.description: A single manuscript-page reproduction embedded between Mather's introduction and edited text, separately scoped from both the physical codex and the complete PMLA scan.

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Digby 185
entities.description: The complete base witness for Mather's edition. The romance occupies ff. 166r-203r within the third of three apparently independent sections of a composite mid-fifteenth-century English codex.

physical_description.extent: ii + 203 + ii leaves; King Ponthus at ff. 166r-203r
notes: Composite of three apparently independent sections; the current description identifies one Anglicana formata scribe for the third section.
The explicit appears on f. 203r, followed by four blank lines; the rest of the leaf has been cut away.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete facsimile of MS Digby 185
entities.description: The current complete 424-surface digital object, from upper board through tail, including a paper slip, flyleaves, every manuscript leaf and adjacent work, boards, spine, fore-edge, head, and tail.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest and 424 JPEG images

entities.label: Digital Bodleian legacy selected-image object for MS Digby 185
entities.description: An older thirteen-image publication from 35mm slide or film sources. It is complete as a capture of the provider's selected object but partial at physical-codex level; only its final three images, all labeled f. 166r, overlap the romance.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest and thirteen selected JPEG images

entities.label: Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Douce 384
entities.description: A factitious composite manuscript of sixty-two leaves. Two nonconsecutive late-fifteenth-century paper leaves at ff. 1r-2v preserve fragments of this English Ponthus; Mather prints Fragment A and Fragment B within his apparatus.

physical_description.support: Paper for the two Ponthus leaves
physical_description.extent: Factitious composite of 62 leaves; Ponthus fragments at ff. 1r-2v
notes: ARLIMA assigns the Ponthus component to Flanders; that witness fact is not used to localize the English translation itself.

entities.label: The Noble History of King Ponthus, Wynkyn de Worde (1511)
entities.description: The later English printed revision or retranslation labeled W by Mather. The Bodleian copy he used is imperfect, lacking A1-2; Mather reports an original extent of one hundred leaves, fifty-four woodcuts, and sixty chapters.

bibliographic.citation: The history of the moost excellent and myghty prince and hygh renowned knight kynge Ponthus of Galyce and lytell Brytaygne (London: Wynkyn de Worde, 1511).
bibliographic.title: The Noble History of King Ponthus

holding.notes: Mather consulted the Bodleian copy; its current shelfmark was not resolved in the public sources checked.

entities.label: Anonymous French prose romance Ponthus et Sidoine
entities.description: The late-fourteenth- or early-fifteenth-century French source work translated and adapted into Middle English. It is an abstract work entity, not any one surviving French manuscript or printed edition.

entities.label: London, British Library, Royal MS 15 E VI
entities.description: Mather's principal French comparison manuscript R. Ponthus et Sidoine occupies ff. 207r-226v inside a 440-folio illustrated compilation made at Rouen in 1444-1445.

physical_description.extent: 440 leaves plus flyleaves; Ponthus et Sidoine at ff. 207r-226v

entities.label: Cambridge University Library, MS Hh.3.16
entities.description: A French Roman de Ponthus manuscript of the first quarter of the fifteenth century, used selectively by Mather as H.

date_statements.label: Manuscript date
date_statements.value: First quarter of the fifteenth century

entities.label: Cambridge University Library, MS Ff.3.31
entities.description: A fifteenth-century paper French Roman de Ponthus manuscript used selectively by Mather as F. The romance begins at f. 1r and is followed by a brief book list on f. 136v.

date_statements.label: Manuscript date
date_statements.value: Third quarter of the fifteenth century

physical_description.extent: 136 leaves; Roman de Ponthus from f. 1r through f. 136v
notes: One column of 25 lines according to the current specialist record.

entities.label: Ponthus et la belle Sydonie, Bodleian Douce 177
entities.description: Gaspard Ortuin's Lyon printed French Ponthus, currently dated c. 1488-1490 and used selectively by Mather as O.

bibliographic.citation: Ponthus et la belle Sydonie (Lyon: Gaspard Ortuin, c. 1488-1490), Bodleian Library Douce 177, Bod-inc. P-443.
bibliographic.title: Ponthus et la belle Sydonie

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete facsimile of Douce 177
entities.description: A complete 156-surface digital object from upper cover through lower cover, including two upper and two lower flyleaves and all printed leaves a1r-l6v.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation manifest and 156 JPEG images

entities.label: Bodleian Quarto Catalogue description of MS Digby 185
entities.description: The historical Macray entry and 1999 addenda, including the codex extent, Ponthus locus and incipit/explicit, dating, and a disputed Hoccleve attribution.

bibliographic.citation: Bodleian Library, Quarto Catalogues, Digby manuscripts, MS 185 entry, printed pp. 195-196, with 1999 addenda p. 85.

entities.label: ARLIMA record for King Ponthus and the Fair Sidone
entities.description: Current specialist work and witness record distinguishing the anonymous English prose romance, Digby and Douce witnesses, French source, Mather edition, and Watson's later work.

agents.name: Henry Watson
notes: Attributed translator of the later printed English version; current records preserve qualification.

agents.name: Archives de littérature du Moyen Âge (ARLIMA)

rights.basis: The work and surviving witness text were created in the fifteenth century. This conclusion does not apply to modern photographs, editorial matter, or digital markup.

rights.component: 1897 editorial text, introduction, apparatus, and typography
rights.basis: The edition was first published in the United States in 1897; U.S. Copyright Office guidance confirms that works published before 1931 are in the public domain as of 2026.

The underlying medieval and 1897 texts are public domain in the United States, but no item-level statement establishing the status of this transcription and markup was captured during the current review.
notes: Public access is not itself a license for redistribution of modern encoding work.

rights.component: Repository-specific corrections, packaging, and later contributions
rights.basis: The checked-in artifact contains public-domain source text and modern repository-level changes whose contribution-by-contribution status is not established here.
The IIIF manifest attributes the photographs to Bodleian Libraries and specifies Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International.
notes: The license is noncommercial and does not authorize for-profit print-on-demand reuse. The medieval text itself is a separate layer.

rights.basis: The legacy IIIF manifest attributes the photographs to Bodleian Libraries and specifies Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International.
The IIIF manifest attributes the photographs to Bodleian Libraries and specifies Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International.
notes: The underlying incunable text is centuries old; this record applies only to the current digital images.

rights.component: Current Cambridge Core PDF reproduction and delivery layer
rights.basis: The reproduced 1897 text is public domain in the United States, but the public PDF's page did not supply a standalone reuse license for the digital file.

editorial_practices.representation: Expanded letters printed in italic type
editorial_practices.description: Mather expands manuscript contractions and marks expansions typographically; the XML carries many of these italic expansions through HI or ABBR structures.
Mather explicitly normalizes capitalization rather than printing it diplomatically.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript with little paragraphing and no modern punctuation system
editorial_practices.description: Mather introduces punctuation and paragraphing for readability while preserving the prose sequence.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript chapter divisions without Mather's numbered prose headings
editorial_practices.description: Mather supplies headings from the text, W, or French versions and brackets editorial matter; the numbered headings are an edition-level navigation system rather than medieval verbatim rubrics.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Digby readings compared with W and French R, H, F, and O sources
editorial_practices.representation: Emendations, supplied matter, variants, and explanatory notes
editorial_practices.description: Square brackets mark supplied editorial matter; parentheses mark emendations from W; changed Digby readings and selected comparison variants appear in notes. W supplies one continuous gap, while other sources are comparative rather than parallel complete streams.
editorial_practices.scope: Edited text and apparatus, printed pp. 1-150

editorial_practices.source_feature: Mather's full contribution: preface, introduction, plate, edited text, notes, and glossary
editorial_practices.representation: Edited Middle English text and notes on printed pp. 1-150
editorial_practices.description: The U-M encoding begins with Mather's edition title and chapter I at printed p. 1 and ends with the explicit on p. 150. It excludes printed pp. i-lxvii and the plate as primary text while retaining the edition's footnote apparatus, including Douce fragments and W-supplied text.

open_questions.question: What evidence underlies the 1999 catalog addendum's attribution of the English romance to Thomas Hoccleve?
next_steps: Review Hunt and Watson's full catalog rationale and modern Hoccleve scholarship.
Keep the romance anonymous and the attribution disputed unless evidence addresses this component independently of neighboring Hoccleve texts.

open_questions.question: Can a complete color surrogate of the entire sixty-two-leaf MS Douce 384 be obtained?
next_steps: Submit a Bodleian mediated-copying or Imaging Services enquiry for the complete factitious composite, including binding, furniture, endleaves, blanks, and every fragment.
Map ff. 1r-2v as the Ponthus portion without reducing the request to those leaves.

open_questions.question: Can the lost French exemplar behind the anonymous English translation be identified or placed more securely among the French textual families?
next_steps: Consult modern stemmatic work on Ponthus et Sidoine and compare it with Mather's provisional R/H/F/O observations.
Do not treat any comparison witness as the direct exemplar without codicological or textual proof.

open_questions.question: What is the current shelfmark and exact surviving physical scope of Mather's Bodleian W copy?
next_steps: Resolve the copy through USTC 501179, ESTC S105285, Bodleian SOLO, and an Early Printed Books enquiry.
If imaging is pursued, request the complete surviving bound object and record the missing A1-2 separately.

open_questions.question: Can complete public or repository-supplied surrogates be secured for Mather's R, H, and F manuscript comparison witnesses?
next_steps: Use the recorded British Library and Cambridge whole-object request routes, preserving all binding matter, blanks, and adjacent contents.
Do not substitute selected illuminations or Mather's cited passages for complete physical-object coverage.
